item.title: NASA chief flew private military jets over D.C. for July 4th despite FAA disapproval
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman flew private military jets over D.C. in a flyover Saturday — with Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche as a passenger — despite safety objections from the FAA.

item.title: Judge orders release of over $5 million due to E. Jean Carroll in Trump case
item.description: The damages were awarded to Carroll, a writer, by a 2023 jury in a civil sexual abuse and defamation case.

item.title: Here's how Democrats could pick a new Maine Senate nominee if Platner drops out
item.description: Democrats have a brief window of time to replace Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner on the ballot if he drops out of the race amid a sexual assault allegation.

item.title: Progressive and moderate Democrats clash in Michigan Senate primary debate
item.description: Michigan state Congresswoman Haley Stevens and former public health official Dr. Abdul El-Sayed did not hold back their personal attacks while squaring off in a tense Senate primary debate on Tuesday. CBS News' Fin Gómez has the details.

item.title: Details on Platner campaign operations amid calls to end Senate bid
item.description: Maine Democrats are calling on Senate nominee Graham Platner to end his campaign ahead of a key ballot deadline. This comes after a woman Platner dated accused him of sexual assault, which he denies. Washington Post Congress reporter Alexei Koseff joins with more.

item.title: Trump licensing Patriot defense systems to Ukraine is a "very big change," expert says
item.description: At least three people are dead in Ukraine following a new wave of Russian missile and drone strikes overnight. On Wednesday, President Trump said the U.S. will license the Patriot missile defense systems to Ukraine for production. Ian Bremmer, the president and founder of the Eurasia Group, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Nolan Wells' family seeks answers after missing Mississippi teen found dead
item.description: The family of Nolan Wells, who was found dead after a July 4 boat trip to Horn Island, has hired civil rights attorney Ben Crump.

item.title: Woman accuses Graham Platner of removing condoms without consent during sex, Washington Post reports
item.description: Graham Platner's Maine Senate campaign was in turmoil on Wednesday as another ex-girlfriend came forward with claims that he removed his condom during sex without consent, according to the Washington Post. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Democratic strategist says Maine Senate race upheaval is "a moment for everybody to take a lesson"
item.description: Maine Democrats are scrambling for an alternative to Graham Platner as they pressure the Senate nominee to drop out of his race. Political strategists Joel Payne and Doug Heye join with analysis.

item.title: Ex-Wisconsin judge fined $5,000, spared prison in ICE obstruction case
item.description: A former Wisconsin judge will soon learn her sentence for felony obstruction for helping an immigrant evade federal officers.

item.title: U.S. launches more Iran strikes after Trump says ceasefire is over
item.description: The U.S. launched a new wave of strikes against Iran Wednesday, hours after President Trump declared the two nations' ceasefire agreement to be over. Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: Smithsonian head disputes White House report accusing institution of "extreme political activism"
item.description: A new White House report accuses the Smithsonian of bias and hostility to White people in its handling of the nation's history. A Washington Post op-ed contends the White House has a bias problem of its own. Its author, Philip Kennicott, senior art and architecture critic for The Post, joins to discuss.

item.title: Second Memphis fatal shooting in 4 days by federal task force, officials say
item.description: A member of a federal crime-fighting task force in Memphis shot and killed a man there on Wednesday, the second fatal shooting by a task force member in four days.

item.title: What to know about "explosive" diarrhea-causing parasite reported in several states
item.description: Michigan is experiencing its largest outbreak of a parasitic infection, called cyclosporiasis, that causes severe diarrhea.

item.title: Highlights from fiery El-Sayed-Stevens Michigan Senate debate
item.description: A progressive Democrat and a moderate Democrat clashed in a tense Senate primary debate in Michigan on Tuesday night. CBS News congressional correspondent Nikole Killion has the latest on the debate between Dr. Abdul El-Sayed and Haley Stevens.

item.title: Why oil prices could go up faster than when Iran war first broke out if renewed fighting lasts long
item.description: Oil prices rose more than 7% to a two-week high after President Trump declared the Iran ceasefire over. Joseph Majkut, director of the Energy Security and Climate Change Program at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, joins with analysis.

item.title: Suspect's roommate in Charlie Kirk killing given immunity for recorded statements
item.description: Prosecutors revealed that the roommate of the man accused of assassinating Charlie Kirk was given "use immunity" in exchange for providing recorded video statements to investigators about the case.

item.title: Platner yet to end Senate bid despite calls from Democrats
item.description: Graham Platner's Senate campaign in Maine has all but collapsed in the wake of a sexual assault allegation that he denies. CBS News congressional correspondent Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Israeli ambassador to the U.N. says "in the long run, we have no intention to stay in Lebanon"
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday said he thought it might be possible that Israeli forces would soon withdraw from southern Lebanon. Israeli Ambassador to the U.N. Danny Danon joins to discuss.

item.title: U.S. launches more strikes against Iran after Trump says ceasefire is "over"
item.description: The U.S. began a second night of strikes against Iranian targets late Wednesday, following Iranian attacks on commercial oil tankers.

item.title: U.S. military launches second night of strikes against Iran
item.description: The U.S. military launched another round of strikes against Iran late Wednesday night, U.S. Central Command said, in the second night of attacks as diplomacy between the two countries appears to collapse.

item.title: Toddler declared dead in pool accident found alive in hospital morgue
item.description: Two police officers saw possible signs of life, but the child was still taken to the hospital's "cold room" after being treated by staff, according to police documents.

item.title: The War and Treaty performs "Litty" at The Great American Block Party 250
item.description: The War and Treaty performs "Litty" from their new album "The Story of Michael and Tanya" at the historic Woolworth Theatre in Nashville.

item.title: Goo Goo Dolls perform "Slide" at The Great American Block Party 250
item.description: The Goo Goo Dolls perform their hit song "Slide" as America rings in its 250th birthday

item.title: 7/8: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: U.S. launches new airstrikes against Iran; Democrats continue calls for Graham Platner to end his campaign.

item.title: Trump says U.S. will grant Ukraine's request to license Patriot defense system
item.description: "A little birdie told me this, about the fact that we'll give them the right to make Patriots," President Trump told Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

item.title: Walmart cuts prices on thousands of items, including beef and soda
item.description: President Trump praised Walmart and said the lower prices are the result of "my Administration's request to celebrate our great Country's 250th birthday."

item.title: A look at Prince Harry's various legal battles against the U.K. media
item.description: Prince Harry has lost his lawsuit against the publisher of the Daily Mail newspaper, but his other legal battles against Britain's tabloids were more successful.

item.title: Philadelphia's Reading Terminal Market offers feast of food, history
item.description: Reading Terminal Market in Philadelphia is just blocks from the location of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. "CBS Saturday Morning" takes a stroll through this historic market.

item.title: How Chef Tim Love helped define the Fort Worth food scene
item.description: Chef Tim Love sits down with "CBS Saturday Morning" to share some of the inspiration for bringing bold flavors to Fort Worth, Texas.
